channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge won't halt Trump, Musk federal worker buyout program
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge George O’Toole found that the unions who had sued over the directive did not have standing to do so.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Tulsi Gabbard Confirmed
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 inspectors general fired by Trump sue to get jobs back
item.description: Eight former inspectors general fired by President Donald Trump are suing to get their jobs back, claiming their terminations were not carried out lawfully.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal health agencies restore webpages and datasets following judge's order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal health agencies have restored several webpages and datasets, following a judge's order to bring back public access to information that had been removed to comply with a presidential executive order.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and KASTURI PANANJADY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kremlin says unidentified Russian freed in U.S. in exchange for Moscow's release of Marc Fogel
item.description: A Kremlin spokesman told reporters that the unidentified individual would return to Russia “in the coming days,” and his name would be revealed when he returns home — unlike during past prisoner exchanges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE takes aim at agency that had plans of regulating X
item.description: Musk and other Silicon Valley executives have long railed against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for its work overseeing the tech industry. Now, Musk hopes to eliminate the agency.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump administration push to reinstate spending freezes on grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Tuesday rejected a Trump administration push to reinstate a sweeping pause on federal funding, a decision that comes after a judge found the administration had not fully obeyed an earlier order. The Boston-based 1st U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Musk appear together in the Oval Office to defend the work of DOGE
item.description: Trump and Musk spoke as the president signed a new executive order calling on the heads of federal agencies to "promptly undertake preparations to initiate large-scale reductions in force.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Judge adjusts ruling blocking Musk, DOGE from Treasury Department payment systems
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday clarified that a ruling preventing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing the Treasury Department’s payment systems does not extend to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmaker moves to put Indigenous Peoples' Day on Columbus Day
item.description: Nevada law says Indigenous Peoples Day falls on Aug. 9, but a lawmaker is trying to change that with AB144, which would set the observation on the second Monday in October — the same day as Colombus Day.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: It’s the last week to change party affiliation ahead of NYC mayor’s race
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Friday is the last day to change your party registration ahead of the 2025 mayoral election. In June, New York City voters will pick their party’s candidate for the mayoral election in November.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Adams thanks DOJ for ending ‘cruel’ bribery case
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday thanked the Department of Justice after an official ordered prosecutors to drop his case, ending what Adams called a “cruel episode.” On Monday, acting Deputy Attorney General Emil Bove directed prosecutors to drop the case against Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Vance to attend international AI and security summits as Ukraine war and tariffs loom
item.description: Vance will likely use the summits to resist further AI regulation while reiterating a common Trump refrain: that military allies need to further ramp up spending that supports NATO and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: A deep dive on U.S. reading and math scores, and what to do about them
item.description: New research drills down on poor student achievement, and notes some school districts that are beating the odds.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical field needs more training like cultural competency, not less, Nevada lawmakers argue
item.description: A proposal to encourage — not require — medical practitioners to get training in subjects ranging from suicide prevention to cultural competency drew an extremely cold reaction from lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from longstanding norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New felony count added to charges against Sen. Nicole Mitchell for April 2024 incident
item.description: Becker County Attorney Brian McDonald added a second burglary charge Monday against state Sen. Nicole Mitchell, DFL-Woodbury, stemming from an incident in April 2024 in Detroit Lakes. Both charges are felonies.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Congress now has a DOGE Caucus. Here's how its co-chair says it will work
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Rep. Pete Sessions, co-chair of the House DOGE Caucus, on how he plans to work with the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Kai McNamee, and Justine Kenin, MPR News

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump's federal worker buyout plan could impact San Diego
item.description: As the deadline looms for workers to accept the Trump administration's sweeping separation offer, the more than two million civilian employees by the federal government across the country are weighing whether to take it.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
